message:
fix(abi): propagate extended attrs (affinity/sigmask) through default thread attr get/set

- Remove redundant pthread_attr_setscope(PTHREAD_SCOPE_SYSTEM) call from
  host_pthread_create_with_managed_attr; Linux only supports SYSTEM scope
  so this was a no-op that could fail on some configurations.
- Add `extended` field (affinity + sigmask) to PthreadAttrDefaults so
  pthread_setattr_default_np persists CPU affinity and signal mask, and
  pthread_getattr_default_np restores them via EXTENDED_ATTR_REGISTRY.
- Derive Clone on ExtendedAttrData and PthreadAttrDefaults to support
  cloning defaults outside the lock scope, avoiding potential deadlock
  when acquiring EXTENDED_ATTR_REGISTRY while DEFAULT_THREAD_ATTR is held.
- Fix pthread_create test to pass null pointer instead of dangling 1-as-ptr
  for destroyed-attr rejection path.
- Add setattr_default_np_preserves_affinity_and_sigmask test covering the
  full roundtrip of affinity + sigmask through default attr set/get.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
